input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition that matches your test project naming convention (e.g. `*.Tests`):

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**Alternatives if naming conventions aren't reliable:**

1. **Check for a sentinel property** set in test `.csproj` files:

```xml
<!-- In each test .csproj -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
</PropertyGroup>
```
```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

2. **Use `Directory.Build.props` only in the test folder** — place a second one in your `tests/` directory that imports the root and adds the MTP properties.

The `EndsWith('.Tests')` approach is the most common since it requires zero changes to individual project files.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants to know how to conditionally set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and MTP runner properties in a shared `Directory.Build.props` so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects).

This is a common pattern when migrating to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that detects test projects — typically by checking for the presence of a test framework package reference or using a naming convention.
